title: utricularia scandens subsp. firmula
intro: Utricularia scandens subsp. firmula, commonly known as the floating bladderwort, is a carnivorous aquatic plant belonging to the bladderwort family (Lentibulariaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Utricularia scandens subsp. firmula include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve boiling or decocting the leaves and stems, followed by the administration of the resulting extract in the form of tea, infusion, or topical application.
